primarySourceLabel: UW Bachelor of Music - Music Education, Instrumental Emphasis
sourceLabel: UW Bachelor of Music - Music Education, Instrumental Emphasis
requirementCueLines: Admission to the Bachelor of Music is intended for especially qualified students who wish to emphasize professional training in music education within a four ‐ year program. Music majors are required to earn a minimum grade of 2.0 in each music course (core and elective) that is to count toward Music degree requirements. A GPA of 2.5 in Music is required for graduation. A minimum of 180 credits is required, of which at least 60 must be taken in departments other than the School of Music. These 60 credits may include courses required to satisfy general education requirements .
MUSIC 113: Beginning Ear Training ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
MUSIC 119: Theory ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 120: Survey of Music History ( Aut/Win/Spr ) (5 credits) or proficiency exam
MUSIC 201, 202: First-Year Theory I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (3,3) (6 credits)
MUSIC 204, 205: First-Year Ear Training I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (1,1) (2 credits)
MUSIC 203, 301, 302: First-Year Theory III & Second-Year Theory I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 206, 304, 305: First-Year Ear Training III & Second Year-Ear Training I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (1,1,1) (3 credits)
MUSIC 210, 211, 212: Music History I, II, III ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 303: Second-Year Theory III ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 306: Second-Year Ear Training III ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
At least 3 credits from among: MUSIC 250, MUSIC 251, MUSIC 252, or any 400-level Ethnomusicology course
TOTAL CORE CREDITS: 36
Additional Music Requirements
MUSIC 380, 381, 382: Instrumental Conducting (1, 1, 1) (3 credits)
MUSED 304, 305: Introductory Music Methods (2, 2) (4 credits)
MUSED 301: Techniques for Teaching Music to Children (2 credits)
MUSED 340: Music in Education (3 credits)
MUSED 452: Ethnomusicology in the Schools (3 credits)
MUSED 442: Instrumental Curriculum: Methods and Materials (3 credits)
MUSED 440: Music for Children (3 credits)
MUSED 465: Classroom Management and Evaluation in Music Education (3 credits)
MUSED 405 or MUSEN 303: Marching Band Techniques or Marching Band (2 credits)
MUSED 403: Part-time Student Teaching (6 credits)
MUSICP 321-339/421/439: Private Instruction (18 credits)
Technique: Junior year: 2 quarters of study in one area: MUSAP 205 (strings), 210 (woodwinds or brass), 217 (percussion), or 218 (guitar) (4 credits)
Technique: Senior year: 2 quarters of study in one area: MUSAP 205 (strings), 210 (woodwinds or brass), 217 (percussion), or 218 (guitar) (4 credits)
Technique: Junior or Senior year: 1 quarter of study in one area: MUSAP 205 (strings), 210 (woodwinds or brass), 217 (percussion), or 218 (guitar) (2 credits)
MUSAP/MUSEN 389, MUSEN 100B, MUSEN 410, or MUSEN 411: World Music, Gospel Choir, Steel Band, or Gamelan (2 credits)
Ensemble Requirements
Participation in at least one School of Music ensemble is required during every quarter of applied music instruction.
Instrumental : MUSEN 300 (Symphony); MUSEN 301 (Wind Ensemble); or MUSEN 302 (Symphonic Band) (6 credits)
Vocal : MUSEN 100, 307, 350, or 351 (1 credit)
Additional Major Requirements
EDC&I 494 (1 credit)
Educational elective (3 credits)
TOTAL NON-MUSIC CREDITS: 60
MUSEC 404: Full-time Student Teaching (15 credits)
See the Degree Program Sheet below for a checklist of course requirements.
